description: Configures MFA methods and enrollment based on risk profile. Implements defense-in-depth: Standard uses convenient push notifications, Elevated adds number challenge to block MFA fatigue attacks, Strict enforces phishing-proof hardware keys.

risk_profile_effects.standard: MFA method: Okta Verify push. WebAuthn disabled.
risk_profile_effects.elevated: MFA method: Okta Verify number challenge (prevents MFA fatigue). WebAuthn enabled.
risk_profile_effects.strict: MFA method: WebAuthn/FIDO2 hardware key (phishing-proof). WebAuthn required.

risk_profile.label: Risk Profile
risk_profile.client_description: Your security tier. Standard works for most companies. Elevated adds protections against MFA fatigue attacks (where hackers spam approval requests). Strict requires physical security keys — best for high-value targets like finance or executive teams.

mfa_method.label: MFA Method
mfa_method.client_description: Override the MFA method set by your risk profile. Options: 'okta_verify_push' — User taps 'Approve' on their phone. Convenient but can be vulnerable to MFA fatigue attacks where attackers spam approval requests. 'okta_verify_number' — User types a matching number from screen into their phone. Prevents MFA fatigue attacks. 'webauthn_fido2' — User taps a physical security key. Completely phishing-proof. Requires hardware keys (~$25-50 each) for all users. Leave blank to use your risk profile default.

enable_webauthn.label: Enable WebAuthn / Hardware Keys
enable_webauthn.client_description: Enable hardware security key support. Required for Elevated and Strict profiles. Standard-only orgs can skip this. If enabled, users can register YubiKeys, Touch ID, or Windows Hello as a second factor.

enable_email_recovery.label: Enable Email Account Recovery
enable_email_recovery.client_description: Allow users to recover their account via email. Recommended for all profiles. Disabling this makes account recovery significantly harder and increases IT helpdesk load.

email_token_lifetime_minutes.label: Email Recovery Link Lifetime (minutes)
email_token_lifetime_minutes.client_description: How long an email recovery link stays valid (in minutes). Default is 5 minutes — short enough to limit exposure if someone intercepts the email, long enough for users to act on it.

outputs.okta_verify_id: Use this to reference Okta Verify in sign-on policies.
outputs.webauthn_id: Use this to reference WebAuthn in sign-on policies. Null if WebAuthn is disabled.
outputs.email_id: Use this to reference Email recovery in authenticator enrollment policies. Null if email recovery is disabled.
outputs.password_id: Use this to reference the Password authenticator in policies.
outputs.effective_mfa_method: The actual MFA method in use after resolving risk profile defaults and any overrides.
